meta_title: For Students: Antigravity project template to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Antigravity Project Template to Solve No Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome project paralysis with the Antigravity template. A beginner-friendly guide to building your first real-world SaaS app using Claude Code.
content.answer_block: The 'no project ideas' trap is a result of overthinking scope. The Antigravity project template provides a production-ready boilerplate that handles authentication, database connectivity, and UI scaffolding. By using Claude Code to automate the heavy lifting, you shift your focus from boilerplate setup to core feature iteration immediately.
content.intro:
The screen stares back, a blinking cursor mocking your ambition. You have the hunger to become a developer, but the blank canvas of a new repository feels like an insurmountable wall. You are trapped in the 'tutorial purgatory,' where the lack of a tangible project idea stalls your transition from student to software engineer.

This is where the Antigravity template changes everything. It is not just code; it is a launchpad designed to bypass the friction of initial setup. By pairing this framework with Claude Code, you transform from a passive learner into an active architect, capable of deploying functional, impressive applications in hours rather than weeks.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Boilerplate
steps.description: Establish your foundation by cloning the core template. This ensures your project structure follows industry-standard security and scalability patterns from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new project using the Antigravity standard stack. Include secure user auth and a PostgreSQL database connection setup.

steps.title: Configure the Database Schema
steps.description: Define your data models. A well-structured schema is the heartbeat of any SaaS application, allowing you to manage user state effectively.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Define a user profile schema including fields for subscription status, bio, and project history.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate prisma schema for user profile and projects'

steps.title: Implement Core Business Logic
steps.description: Write the unique functionality that makes your app valuable. Focus on solving one specific problem for the user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an API route that calculates project progress based on completed tasks in the database.

steps.title: Build the Responsive Frontend
steps.description: Create the interface. Use Antigravity's component library to ensure your UI is accessible and aesthetically professional.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a dashboard component that displays project metrics using the Antigravity UI library.

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Move your code from your local machine to the cloud. This step solidifies your learning by putting your app in front of real users.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment configuration for Vercel/Railway including environment variable setup.

pro_tips: Always commit your changes before asking Claude Code to refactor to ensure you can revert if the logic shifts.
Treat your README.md as a product roadmap; update it every time you finish a feature to build a portfolio-ready history.
Use environment variables for all API keys, even in early dev, to build good security habits early.
Focus on one 'hero' feature rather than five mediocre ones to make your portfolio project stand out.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your core logic; it teaches you how to think about edge cases like a senior dev.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share your live URL in the community feedback channel. Document your challenges in a blog post to demonstrate your problem-solving process to future employers.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build, the tools to automate, and the roadmap to finish. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the one you have today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
